codeReview.summary: The reviewed feature does not contain a scoped implementation for the stated editor auth-navigation regression. The worker only reran tests and attached unrelated commit `568e34d`, while the actual auth-helper stabilization lives in earlier commits outside this feature, so the feature does not satisfy its description as an implementation unit.

issues.description: The handoff records commit `568e34d` as the fix, but `git show --stat 568e34d` changes only `apps/website/e2e/timer-page.spec.ts`. It does not touch `apps/website/e2e/time-entry-editor.real-runtime.spec.ts` or `apps/website/e2e/fixtures/e2e-auth.ts`, so the attached fix commit is unrelated to the editor auth-navigation regression.

This feature returned success without producing a feature-scoped implementation. The handoff says the issue was already resolved by earlier commits (`7bd0687`, `a7f3363`, `4fcb0d3`, `8260520`), but those commits predate this feature and mostly add/remove temporary debug logging around `registerE2eUser`; they are not tied to this feature handoff and do not create an auditable implementation unit for `tracking-time-entry-editor-auth-navigation-fix`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `frontend-test-worker` procedure is still being bypassed in practice for rerun-only flaky-fix work: this worker claimed success after green reruns without proving that the reported commit directly contained the stabilization change.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/frontend-test-worker/SKILL.md` step 10 requires verifying that the reported commit directly contains the feature's code changes, and step 11 forbids treating a rerun-only pass as completion for a flaky-fix feature. The transcript skeleton for worker `958fdf47-fd52-41e7-895a-b616f1796d55` shows only test reruns and git inspection before the handoff attached unrelated commit `568e34d`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The repo-level shared state documents the `frontend-entry-editor` lane but does not capture that the auth-helper stabilization for this lane currently lives in `apps/website/e2e/fixtures/e2e-auth.ts` and may already be satisfied by earlier commits, which made this worker spend time rediscovering history instead of reviewing one canonical fix unit.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/testing-lanes.md` lists the `frontend-entry-editor` lane, while `git log -- apps/website/e2e/time-entry-editor.real-runtime.spec.ts apps/website/e2e/fixtures/e2e-auth.ts` shows the only auth-helper stabilization in recent history is commit `94f7496` on `e2e-auth.ts`, not the feature's attached commit `568e34d`.

summary: Fail: the feature handoff proves the tests are green but does not implement or accurately reference the editor auth-navigation fix. The attached commit is unrelated to the editor lane, so scrutiny cannot accept this feature as the requested repair.
